The rotation mechanism allows rotating the string after a node `?n` by `?r`.
The action `rotate` takes `?n`, `?r`, `?fromdir`, `?todir`.
It changes the heading of `?n` from `?fromdir` to `?todir`.
It sets up the first pass cursor `node-first-pass-next ?n ?r ?n`.
Then `rotate-first-pass` actions propagate the rotation to subsequent nodes.
Then `rotate-first-pass-end` finishes the first pass.
Then `rotate-second-pass` actions propagate the coordinates.
Then `rotate-second-pass-end` finishes the second pass.

Wait, looking at the domain description:
"(:action rotate
    :parameters (?n - node ?r - rotation ?fromdir ?todir - direction)
    :precondition
        (and
            (not (rotating))
            (NEXT-DIRECTION ?fromdir ?r ?todir)
            (heading ?n ?fromdir)
        )
    :effect
        (and
            (not (heading ?n ?fromdir))
            (heading ?n ?todir)
            (rotating)
            (node-first-pass-next ?n ?r ?n)
            (increase (total-cost) (rotate-cost))
        )
)"

This action rotates the heading of node `?n`.
It initiates the process.
The subsequent actions `rotate-first-pass` and `rotate-second-pass` handle the rest of the string.
The `rotate-first-pass` action updates the heading of the next node `?n2` based on the rotation `?r` applied to the previous node `?n1`.
Wait, the parameters for `rotate-first-pass` are `?nstart`, `?r`, `?n1`, `?n2`, `?n2x`, `?n2y`, `?n2dir`, `?n2setdir`.
It requires `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`.
It requires `node-first-pass-next ?nstart ?r ?n1`.
It updates `?n2`'s heading to `?n2setdir` (which is `NEXT-DIRECTION ?n2dir ?r ?n2setdir`).
It removes `?n2` from its current position `?n2x ?n2y` and marks it free.
It sets `node-first-pass-next ?nstart ?r ?n2`.
So it propagates the "next node to process" cursor.

The `rotate-first-pass-end` action is triggered when the last node is reached.
It requires `END-NODE ?n2` and `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`.
It removes the cursor and sets `node-second-pass-next ?nstart`.

Then `rotate-second-pass` updates the coordinates.
It requires `CONNECTED ?n1 ?n2`.
It requires `node-second-pass-next ?n1`.
It checks `at ?n1 ?n1x ?n1y` and `heading ?n1 ?n1dir`.
It checks `free ?n2x ?n2y`.
It calculates `?n2x ?n2y` based on `?n1`'s position and heading.
It sets `at ?n2 ?n2x ?n2y`.
It sets `node-second-pass-next ?n2`.

Finally `rotate-second-pass-end` clears the cursor and `rotating`.

So a full rotation involves:
1. `rotate` at the pivot node.
2. Sequence of `rotate-first-pass` for all subsequent nodes.
3. `rotate-first-pass-end`.
4. Sequence of `rotate-second-pass` for all subsequent nodes.
5. `rotate-second-pass-end`.
